primary_catalyst.description: AI spending sustainability concerns intensify; NVDA continues slide from Oct 29 all-time high amid worries about hyperscaler capex peak, GPU depreciation accounting at hyperscalers, and custom-silicon competition from Google/Amazon. Broad sector de-rating extends.

secondary_catalysts.description: Bloomberg: 'Nvidia's $4 Trillion Stock Rally Faces More Threats Than Ever' framing piece published Jan 6, continues to weigh on sentiment
Seeking Alpha ratings downgrade (Jan 2026) amplifying AI capex peak narrative

force_attributions.rationale: Hyperscaler capex peak concern dominated the tape; market re-rating on whether 60%+ capex growth is sustainable
Custom silicon displacement threat (TPU, Trainium, MTIA) cited directly in the CNBC piece
Position unwind from mega-cap concentration; stock down ~9% from Oct 29 highs

notes: Move is part of a multi-week narrative re-evaluation, not a single catalyst. CES 2026 product announcements (Rubin, DRIVE AV) failed to halt the slide.
